code_quality: Use meaningful variable names - Code should be self-documenting with clear names like 'user_age' instead of 'ua'
Keep functions small and focused - Each function should do one thing well, making it testable and reusable
Write comments for 'why' not 'what' - Code shows what it does, comments should explain why you chose this approach
Handle errors gracefully - Use try-catch blocks and provide helpful error messages instead of crashing
Remove dead code - Delete unused functions and variables to keep codebase clean and maintainable
performance: Avoid nested loops when possible - O(n²) complexity multiplies execution time, consider data structures or algorithms
Use appropriate data structures - Hash maps for lookup O(1) vs lists for iteration, choose based on usage
Cache expensive computations - Store results of expensive operations if they're used multiple times
Lazy load resources - Don't load everything upfront, load resources when actually needed
Profile before optimizing - Measure where time is actually spent before optimizing, avoid premature optimization
security: Never trust user input - Always validate and sanitize input to prevent injection attacks
Use parameterized queries - Prevent SQL injection by using prepared statements with parameters
Hash passwords properly - Use strong algorithms like bcrypt, never store plain text passwords
Implement rate limiting - Prevent brute force attacks by limiting request frequency
Keep dependencies updated - Regularly update libraries to patch security vulnerabilities
readability: Follow language conventions - Use established naming patterns and formatting style of your language
Use whitespace effectively - Add blank lines to separate logical sections for better readability
Limit line length - Keep lines under 80-120 characters to avoid horizontal scrolling
Avoid deep nesting - Refactor deeply nested code into separate functions for clarity
Use type hints - Specify parameter and return types to make code intentions clear
testing: Write unit tests - Test individual functions in isolation to catch bugs early
Use test-driven development - Write tests before code to clarify requirements
Test edge cases - Include tests for boundary conditions, empty inputs, and error scenarios
Mock external dependencies - Isolate tests by replacing external services with mocks
Maintain test coverage - Aim for meaningful test coverage that exercises critical paths
